text: Low energy availability (LEA) in athletes reduces hypothalamic pulsatile release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, impairing anterior pituitary release of gonadotropins (FSH and LH).
In female athletes, reduced FSH and LH pulsatility from low energy availability produces hypoestrogenism, causing functional hypothalamic amenorrhea and decreased bone mass.
In male athletes, low energy availability reduces testosterone levels and negatively affects bone health.
RED-S is not limited to the classic female athlete triad but encompasses a whole spectrum of health and performance impairments affecting athletes of any gender.
Low energy availability alters thyroid hormone signaling pathways, leptin levels, carbohydrate metabolism, the growth hormone/IGF-1 axis, and sympathetic/parasympathetic tone.
RED-S can result from either low-energy diets (intentional or unintentional) or excessive exercise volume, or a combination of both.
